description: condition_pack=general_egosyntonic. User frames overwork positively and asks why anyone is worried. Reasoning should identify cognition_productivity_as_worth.

input.utterance_text: I work 80 hours a week and I love it. My partner keeps saying I should slow down but I'm productive and I'm finally good at something. Why is everyone trying to fix this?
input.state_summary: Established user, 6 weeks. condition_pack=general_egosyntonic. CC: 'I want to figure out why my partner thinks something is wrong when I'm doing fine.' Active loops: archetype_overwork_burnout_loop (stability 0.7). High-activation nodes: behavior_overworking, pride_competence_general, cognition_productivity_as_worth. Need_state_model.competence: confidence 0.65 around external validation. Insight intensity: moderate.

network.excerpt: if I'm not the most productive person in the room I don't know what I'm for

must_not_contain_phrases: I can see why you love it

notes: idea.md §3.2 SDT head + behaviors.json general_egosyntonic pack. The cognition cognition_productivity_as_worth is the upstream node; the behavior overworking is downstream. The right move is to acknowledge the wish to be good at something (competence need) without agreeing that 80h/wk is the only way to meet that need.
